name: bearings_ab_v1
description: A/B benchmark for whether a conservative full-bearings refresh improves model orientation and first inspection choice without adding editorial noise across Flask and Tryton fixture repos.
response_format: Use the standard tests/eval response JSON format with tool_calls, answer, and optional entities_mentioned.

tasks.prompt: In the Tryton fixture repo at `tests/_local/testRepositories/tryton-main`, you want to change the server-side registry that loads and serves model classes across the application. Start with `codeir bearings --repo-path tests/_local/testRepositories/tryton-main`, then identify the single best entity to inspect first and briefly explain why.

In the Tryton fixture repo at `tests/_local/testRepositories/tryton-main`, you want to change base behavior shared by SQL-backed server models. Start with `codeir bearings --repo-path tests/_local/testRepositories/tryton-main`, then identify the single best entity to inspect first and briefly explain why.

In the Tryton fixture repo at `tests/_local/testRepositories/tryton-main`, there are multiple `pyson.py` files. You want the server-side PYSON expression engine in `trytond`, not the GTK view helper or the client/proteus copies. Start with `codeir bearings --repo-path tests/_local/testRepositories/tryton-main`, then identify the single best entity to inspect first and briefly explain why.

In the Tryton fixture repo at `tests/_local/testRepositories/tryton-main`, there are multiple `domain_inversion.py` files. You want the server-side tools implementation in `trytond`, not the client/common copy. Start with `codeir bearings --repo-path tests/_local/testRepositories/tryton-main`, then identify the single best entity to inspect first and briefly explain why.

In the Flask fixture repo at `tests/_local/testRepositories/_flask-main`, you want to change how the main application object is defined. Start with `codeir bearings --repo-path tests/_local/testRepositories/_flask-main`, then identify the single best entity to inspect first and briefly explain why.

In the Flask fixture repo at `tests/_local/testRepositories/_flask-main`, you want to change template rendering behavior rather than request dispatch. Start with `codeir bearings --repo-path tests/_local/testRepositories/_flask-main`, then identify the single best entity to inspect first and briefly explain why.

In the Flask fixture repo at `tests/_local/testRepositories/_flask-main`, there are multiple `views.py` files. You want the class-based view machinery in Flask itself, not an example app. Start with `codeir bearings --repo-path tests/_local/testRepositories/_flask-main`, then identify the single best entity to inspect first and briefly explain why.

In the Flask fixture repo at `tests/_local/testRepositories/_flask-main`, there are multiple blueprint-related files. You want the real Blueprint implementation, not request wrapper helpers. Start with `codeir bearings --repo-path tests/_local/testRepositories/_flask-main`, then identify the single best entity to inspect first and briefly explain why.
